sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a collar, neckchain, necklace, Son 4:9 pl. ־ִים and ֹות Pro 1:9 Jud. 8:26 .

(2) i.q. Arab. عَنَقُ length of neck and stature (compare أَعْنَقُ long-necked); hence בְּנֵי עֲנָק, בְּנֵּי הָעֲנָק Num 13:33 יְלִידֵי הָעֲנָק ibid. verses 22, 28; also עֲנָקִים Deu 1:28 2:10 , 11 , 21 , and בְּנֵי עֲנָקִים Deu 9:2. The Anakim (prop. men with long necks, of high stature), pr.n. of a Canaanite nation, famous on account of their height, who inhabited Hebron previous to the Hebrews taking possession of the land ( Jos 11:21); they were almost utterly extirpated by them, but a few remained in the cities of the Philistines (compare the interpreters and critics on Jer 47:5).
